lawrence.price_source: Stooq/Yahoo 7176.JP last print 2026-01-07 (¥464, 100 shares); not a continuous liquid quote

nowcast.aum_source: filing sleeves MTM (non-listed Nikkei + ETF basket); +company ETF flow YTD ¥277m

nowcast_v5_jpym.note: Live v5 uses v1 perf proxy until mandate detail refreshed for current half

caveats: Perf fee crystallizes near fiscal year-end (Mar); H1 nowcast of perf is low-confidence.
v2 uses fund-proxy excess when panel has it; live nowcast uses 0.65*Nikkei + 0.35*value.
AUM uses filing non-listed + ETF sleeves, not summed product-market AUM.
Market return from cached_market_nav; verify before trading on nowcast.

base_fee.note: Effective base rate ~0.5-0.56%/yr, mild upward drift (mix shift to higher-fee active/ETF).

perf_fee.form: perf_fee_half = k_half * avg_AUM * max(0, nikkei_ret - hurdle)
perf_fee.note: k_H2 >> k_H1: performance fees crystallize at the March fiscal year-end. This seasonality (H2 revenue ~2-3x H1) is the core forecastable structure.

spec_leaderboard.note: v2 worse on revenue OOS RMSE
v3b + march + split base + AUM roll-forward

caveats: Primary KPI: perf_fee_h2_positive OOS R² (not total-revenue IS R²).
perf_fee_h2_positive OOS n is small (fee split disclosed only from FY2024).
production_spec=v5 per acceptance rule (lowest perf_fee H2 OOS RMSE not worse than v1).
Naive same-half-last-year beats model on revenue OOS RMSE (¥3,230m vs ¥4,336m).
n≈12 half-years; negative OOS R² is valid (model worse than mean).

leaderboard.note: v2 worse on revenue OOS RMSE
v3b + march + split base + AUM roll-forward

spec_comparison.acceptance_rule: Reject spec if perf_fee_h2_oos_rmse worsens vs prior

triangulation: Lawrence IRR uses mid-cycle owner cash (FY2025 ¥137/sh), not peak FY2026 success-fee earnings.
High synthesis return is not executable on the public TPM tape at last thin print.
Model edge is performance-fee seasonality and pre-report nowcast, not beating seasonal naive on RMSE.
caveats: Out-of-sample RMSE is worse than same-half-last-year naive; use model for seasonality and scenarios, not level beats.
Perf fee crystallizes near fiscal year-end (Mar); H1 nowcast of perf is low-confidence.
v2 uses fund-proxy excess when panel has it; live nowcast uses 0.65*Nikkei + 0.35*value.
AUM uses filing non-listed + ETF sleeves, not summed product-market AUM.
Market return from cached_market_nav; verify before trading on nowcast.

portfolio.disclaimer: Roth IRA paper portfolio / research tool. Not tax or investment advice.

portfolio_explanation.summary: We hold 9 names under **ira_marvin** in a **calm** regime. IRA Marvin policy ranks names using Marvin deep-dive IRR, moat, dhando, staleness, and falsifier penalties, then applies mandate caps (max weight, min IRR, stance). Largest positions: CSU 15.0%, FRMO 15.0%, QDEL 13.25%.
portfolio_explanation.mechanism: IRA Marvin policy ranks names using Marvin deep-dive IRR, moat, dhando, staleness, and falsifier penalties, then applies mandate caps (max weight, min IRR, stance).

drivers.role: Primary rank input for IRA / IRR policies
Score multipliers in policy genomes
Book total 0; per-name penalties
0 names with deep dive older than review threshold
Max weight, min names, turnover, stance caps
Label calm adjusts constraint overrides
